join_order.build: sub(sic[], cik[]) — full arrays loaded; adsh_qualifying_bitset and adsh_to_cik_idx built
join_order.notes: sub_sic (172KB) and sub_cik (344KB) fully loaded. adsh_qualifying_bitset = bitset<86135> (~10KB, L1-resident): set bit i if sub_sic[i] BETWEEN 4000-4999. adsh_to_cik_idx[i] = cik_remap_index (uint16_t) for qualifying adsh_codes, UINT16_MAX otherwise.
tag_abstract (1MB) fully loaded; O(1) by tagver_code; abstract==0 ~95% pass

join_order.build: eq_count_map (FlatCountMap, 65536 slots, 786KB) — SIC-pre-filtered EQ pairs only
join_order.notes: Pre scan filters by adsh_qualifying_bitset AND stmt==eq_code: ~42K keys. FlatCountMap cap_hint=25000 -> 65536 slots x 12B = 786KB (L2/L3-resident). NO bloom filter — with 42K entries in 65536 slots, direct probe is cache-resident and FPR of any bloom would be ~0%, making bloom computation pure waste.

filters.notes: Zone maps skip entire 100K-row blocks; row-level check applied first
Most selective — applied after uom+tagver checks via O(1) bitset lookup (adsh_qualifying_bits, ~10KB, L1-resident)
Direct eq_count_map probe (786KB, L2/L3-resident). NO bloom filter step.

predicate_order_per_num_row: 1. uom_code == usd_code (zone map at block level, then row check)

data_structures.sub_sic_array: flat_array(86135 x int16_t) — direct indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.sub_cik_array: flat_array(86135 x int32_t) — direct indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.adsh_to_cik_idx: flat_array(86135 x uint16_t = 172KB): maps qualifying adsh_code -> CIK remap index [0, N_ciks); UINT16_MAX for non-qualifying
data_structures.tag_abstract_array: flat_array(1070662 x int8_t) — direct indexed by tagver_code
data_structures.bloom_filter: ELIMINATED — 42K entries in 65536-slot FlatCountMap means direct probe is L2/L3-resident; bloom adds 7 hash computations per row with ~0% FPR benefit; pure overhead removed
data_structures.thread_local_agg: FlatAggMap — custom SoA open-addressing hash map with NO per-group heap allocation. Layout: keys[cap](uint64_t), sums[cap](double), cnts[cap](int64_t), cik_data[cap * cik_words](uint64_t flat). All pre-allocated at construction, zeroed once. No vector<uint64_t> pointer indirection per group.
data_structures.thread_agg_cap: cap=1024 for worker threads (per thread: 8+8+8+442 = 466KB, L3-resident; 64 threads x 466KB = 29.8MB, fits in 44MB LLC). cap=8192 for thread_maps[0] (accumulates all groups: 3.73MB, L3-resident).
data_structures.aggregation: FlatAggMap per thread; merge via parallel binary-tree reduction (6 rounds for 64 threads). CIK merge = iterate src.keys array (8KB, L1-resident), for each occupied slot s: OR cik_data[d*cw..d*cw+cw] |= cik_data[s*cw..s*cw+cw].

flat_agg_map_spec.description: Custom SoA open-addressing hash map eliminating double heap indirection
flat_agg_map_spec.construction: Allocate all arrays once; keys filled with EMPTY; sums/cnts/cik_data zeroed. No per-group allocation ever.
flat_agg_map_spec.find_or_insert: hash(key) & mask -> linear probe keys[] -> return slot index
flat_agg_map_spec.merge_from_src: for s in [0,src.cap): if src.keys[s]!=EMPTY: d=find_or_insert(src.keys[s]); sums[d]+=src.sums[s]; cnts[d]+=src.cnts[s]; for j in [0,cw): cik_data[d*cw+j] |= src.cik_data[s*cw+j]

load_sub_arrays_and_build_cik_remap: Build adsh_qualifying_bits[86135] (~10KB): for i in [0,SUB_N): if sub_sic[i] BETWEEN 4000-4999, set bit i
Build cik_to_idx map and idx_to_cik vector: for each qualifying i, assign remap index to sub_cik[i]
Build adsh_to_cik_idx[86135]: uint16_t, UINT16_MAX for non-qualifying; qualifying i -> cik_remap_index

build_eq_count_map_with_sic_prefilter.strategy: parallel scan; thread-local key vectors; single-threaded insert into FlatCountMap
build_eq_count_map_with_sic_prefilter.per_thread_filter: if (stmt==eq_code) AND (adsh_qualifying_bits[ac] is set): emit key=((uint64_t)ac<<32)|(uint32_t)tagver; skip if key==UINT64_MAX
build_eq_count_map_with_sic_prefilter.merge_into_map: single-threaded insert of ~42K keys into 786KB FlatCountMap (<1ms)
build_eq_count_map_with_sic_prefilter.no_bloom_build: Do NOT build any bloom filter

aggregation.strategy: FlatAggMap per thread (SoA, no heap allocation per group) + parallel binary-tree reduction
accumulators_in_flat_map: cik_data[slot * cik_words + (cik_idx >> 6)]: uint64_t bitset word — OR-set bit (cik_idx & 63)
aggregation.cik_insert: cik_data[slot * cik_words + (ci >> 6)] |= 1ULL << (ci & 63)  // O(1), no allocation, no pointer follow
aggregation.merge_strategy: parallel binary-tree reduction (6 rounds for 64 threads). For each src slot: find dst slot; sums[d]+=src.sums[s]; cnts[d]+=src.cnts[s]; OR cik_data words. No heap allocation, no pointer following.
aggregation.having: popcount over cik_data[slot * cik_words .. slot * cik_words + cik_words] >= 2
aggregation.post_having_decode: iterate global FlatAggMap (thread_maps[0]); for each non-empty slot: decode tlabel from tlabel_offsets+tlabel_data[tagver_code]; re-aggregate by (sic, tlabel) string key with bitwise-OR CIK merge; then popcount for final count; HAVING+sort+limit

parallelism.merge_strategy: parallel binary-tree reduction (6 rounds for 64 threads); CIK merge is inline bitwise OR over cik_data flat array — O(cik_words) per group, no heap allocation

indexes.usage: skip block b if zm[b].min_uom > usd_code || zm[b].max_uom < usd_code; struct ZoneMap { int8_t min_uom, max_uom; int32_t min_ddate, max_ddate; } sizeof=12 (2-byte pad)

output.tlabel_decode: after primary FlatAggMap tree-merge, iterate global map slots; decode tlabel for each non-empty slot from tlabel_offsets+tlabel_data by tagver_code; re-aggregate by (sic, tlabel) string into a secondary unordered_map; CIK merge via bitwise OR; popcount for final cik count; HAVING cik_count>=2; sort by total_value DESC; limit 500

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category B — algorithm change): iter_2 regressed main_scan from 49ms to 84ms and merge_aggregates from 36ms to 42ms despite a smaller eq_count_map. The culprit is std::unordered_map<uint64_t, AggState> where AggState embeds a vector<uint64_t> cik_bits. This creates two levels of heap indirection on every aggregation operation: (1) the unordered_map node is heap-allocated with linked-list chaining, and (2) AggState.cik_bits is a heap-allocated 432-byte array (assign(54, 0) triggered on each new group). With ~100 new groups per thread, this produces ~100 small allocations scattered across the heap, making every subsequent cik_data access a random cache miss. The fix is a custom FlatAggMap using SoA layout — keys[cap], sums[cap], cnts[cap], cik_data[cap*cik_words] — where all arrays are pre-allocated and zeroed at construction. No per-group heap allocation ever occurs; the cik_data access is cik_data[slot*cik_words+word] with no pointer indirection. Memory: cap=1024 per worker thread → 466KB/thread; 64 threads = 29.8MB total (fits in 44MB LLC). Thread_maps[0] uses cap=8192 (3.73MB, L3-resident) to accumulate all groups after tree-reduction. Additionally, the 256KB bloom filter is eliminated: with ~42K entries probing a 786KB FlatCountMap, the FPR is ~10^-7 (essentially zero), so the bloom's 7 hash computations per qualifying row produce no filtering benefit — pure wasted cycles. Expected: main_scan ~35ms, merge_aggregates ~8ms, total ~65ms.
